data.content:
Good news — in .NET 10, `dotnet test` uses Microsoft.Testing.Platform **by default**. The `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` property was only needed in .NET 9 to opt in; on .NET 10 it's redundant and can be removed.

You still need `EnableMSTestRunner` (that's what makes MSTest produce an MTP executable). Here's the cleanup:
data.reasoningText:
The user is on .NET 10 SDK (10.0.100) and is using MSTest with Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP) via `EnableMSTestRunner` and `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`.

In .NET 10, `dotnet test` natively uses Microsoft.Testing.Platform. The `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` property was needed in .NET 9 to opt into the MTP-based `dotnet test` experience. In .NET 10, this is the default behavior, so `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` is no longer needed.


Since .NET 10 has MTP built in by default, I should remove that property from the project file.

data.content: Let me clean up that blank line:

**Summary:**

- **`TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`** — removed. Not needed on .NET 10; MTP is the default `dotnet test` integration.
- **`EnableMSTestRunner`** — keep it. This tells MSTest to build as an MTP executable (`OutputType: Exe`).
- **No other changes needed.** Just run `dotnet test` as usual — it will use MTP natively.

If you ever need to fall back to the legacy VSTest integration, you can set `<TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>false</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>`, but that shouldn't be necessary going forward.
